channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Gray Zone Warfare’s Next Few Updates Have Been Teased
item.content:encoded:
At the tail end of last month, Gray Zone Warfare’s 0.4 update, otherwise known as Spearhead, was released. This gargantuan overhaul was dubbed a ‘new game, new experience’ by MADFINGER Games, and it touched every single aspect of the open-world sandbox shooter.

In a new video, MADFINGER explained what’s coming in the next few updates to Gray Zone Warfare. Plenty is coming, and the team plans to iterate quickly, responding to the community’s biggest concerns and further optimizing the game beyond Spearhead.

Gray Zone Warfare Continues to Grow

In my recent review, I awarded Gray Zone Warfare high marks based on the evolution of the title going into the 0.4 update. It’s night and day compared to the version available at launch in April 2024, and the game continues to blow the minds of tactical shooter fans worldwide.

Here’s a snippet from that review:

Gray Zone Warfare represents a stunning example of what happens when a dedicated, devoted developer expresses nothing but passion for the core product they have created. It’s without a doubt a best-in-class title that has untold amounts of potential in store, and after the 0.4 update, it’s an open-world tactical shooter that simply must be experienced to be believed.

In a new video uploaded to the MADFINGER channel on YouTube, the team has revealed that multiple updates are planned for the coming weeks. This will introduce features such as a brand-new player visibility indicator, which will let you know how well enemies can hear and see you.

The smaller locations in the world will have tweaks when it comes to roaming AI enemies, and large locations will have their NPC volume boosted to account for the size and scale of the POIs. Further amendments are also being made to enemy difficulty ratings across Lamang.

Gray Zone’s task system, in-game economy, and vendor systems are all being updated, based on critical feedback received when Spearhead went nuclear and pulled in tens of thousands of players.

For NVIDIA users, DLSS 4.5 is coming in Q2 2026, which will offer a nice technical boost.

It was then revealed that, to celebrate the game’s second anniversary, a special in-game event will be hosted with the community, in addition to a substantial sale on the game’s website.

Are you playing Gray Zone Warfare? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Escape from Tarkov players have found a mystery item

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dark Souls 2 Modders Are Beta Testing Path Tracing
item.content:encoded:
Dark Souls Lighting Engine has released a path tracing mode for DS2 in public beta.

FromSoftware originally released Dark Souls 2 on the PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360, and later released an enhanced version for PS4, Xbox One, and PC. However, since then, no updates have arrived, which means the vanilla versions of the game cannot take advantage of lighting features available on today’s machines. Now, thanks to modders, players can enjoy one of these features on PC.

Dark Souls 2 Path Tracing Mod is Available Now

A post by the official Dark Souls Lighting Engine account on X (formerly Twitter) reveals that their Path Tracing mod has entered public beta. Interested users can try it out through their Discord until they are ready to upload it to Nexus Mods. The mod helps “hide texture tiling issues and also adds contrast from lighting, breaking up the monotony of surfaces.” Interested readers can check out a comparison video for the path tracing mod below:

For those unaware, Path Tracing is an advanced rendering technique often referred to as ‘full ray tracing.’ It uses an algorithm that simulates the true physics of light, which uses ray tracing as one component of a larger light simulation system. Interested readers can learn more about the technical aspects of this feature through Nvidia’s blog.

In other news, a new Bloodborne movie is in the works, and it has a famous content creator, Jacksepticeye, helping develop it. Additionally, Crimson Desert developers have revealed that they are discussing ideas for official mod support internally. What are your thoughts on the path tracing mode for Dark Souls 2? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Xbox teasing a new collaboration with Discord. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Xbox Teases New Collaboration with Discord
item.content:encoded:
Xbox CEO has stated that they have something new in the works with Discord to make Game Pass more flexible.

Talking about Game Pass, a report from earlier today reveals that their long-term plan is to make the service more flexible, with a ‘pick your own plan’ formula under consideration. Now, in their effort to bring more flexibility, a collaboration with Discord is in the works, with more details coming soon.

Xbox Game Pass May Include Discord Nitro in the Future

A new post by Xbox CEO Asha Sharma on X (formerly Twitter) announced the new collaboration with Discord. The post states that the two teams will partner again as they continue to make Game Pass more flexible. The post added that some players “might start to see some code in the wild,” with more details about this arriving soon. Players are already speculating that Discord Nitro could be bundled with Xbox Game Pass in the future.

The idea of new bundled services is not far-fetched, as Xbox is already testing tiers internally with varying offerings. A Triton Tier is in the works that could provide access to only first-party games on Game Pass. Another one, called Duet, is also in the works and could offer a bundled subscription with Netflix. Discord could have a similar collab, where players could get Nitro with Game Pass Ultimate or vice versa. However, that part is yet to be confirmed.

In other news, Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has stated that the idea of an Xbox Mobile Store is not dead yet. Additionally, the last collaboration brought the ability to view Discord streams directly from consoles. What are your thoughts on the upcoming collaboration between Xbox and Discord? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the 007 First Light Rules of Spycraft trailer showcasing new gameplay. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: 007 First Light Trailer Showcases New Gameplay
item.content:encoded:
007 First Light has received a brand new trailer showcasing fresh gameplay and the ‘Rules of Spycraft’.

IO Interactive’s Bond-themed adventure is all set to arrive next month, and the developer has already provided a whole bunch of details ahead of the release. Just last week, we received the 007 First Light title sequence, featuring a cinematic Bond-style intro and the theme song by Lana Del Rey. Now, a new trailer has arrived showcasing more gameplay.

007 First Light’s Rules of Spycraft Trailer Reveals Stealth Gameplay, Gadgets, Tac Sim, and More

Blending In

The new trailer highlights the various gameplay features players will explore as a spy in the world of 007 First Light. The first aspect highlighted was blending in, where players will have to explore different areas to infiltrate, bluff their way past security guards, and eavesdrop on conversations for more intel. The trailer also highlights various stealth features, such as sneaking around and takedowns, as well as the parkour paths players can take to infiltrate.

Gadgets

However, bluffing might help you in every situation, which brings players to the second rule of Spycraft, which is to always be prepared for the worst. 007 First Light players will have time between missions to talk to allies, gather intel, and prepare for the next mission. The gadgets lab was also highlighted, showcasing various tools players can use, including the Q-Lens, the Q-Watch, the Dart Phone, the Smoke Pod, earbuds, and more.

Improvise

The next highlighted section was improvisation, where players can use elements of the environment to their advantage. This includes tossing items at enemies, lunging over obstacles, and pushing enemies into environmental elements. Players also have the license to kill, so use your guns when you end up in sticky situations.

Tac Sim

The new 007 First Light trailer also highlighted various types of enemies players will face. Some might have additional armor that will need to be handled with your gadgets. The Focus ability was also showcased, allowing Bond to slow time for a few seconds to take precision shots. There’s also a Tac Sim that players can use to test their abilities against others while earning experience for upgrades. Tac Sim will also receive new content over time.

In other news, A security mishap at the Indonesian ratings board has led to 007 First Light spoilers appearing online. Additionally, the bright gold 007 First Light Limited Edition DualSense Wireless controller has been revealed. What are your thoughts on the new 007 First Light Gameplay trailer? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Xbox CEO stating that the idea of a mobile store is not dead. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
